The Monday Clinical Brief ← Back to homepage

Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 2026

The Monday Clinical Brief is a weekly journal digest service for medical professionals. We are committed to protecting your personal data and being transparent about what we collect and why.

Who we are

The Monday Clinical Brief is operated by Tim Hamilton, based in Wales, United Kingdom. If you have any questions about this policy or your data, you can contact us at info@mondayclinicalbrief.co.uk.

What data we collect

We collect the minimum data necessary to provide our service:

  • Email address — to deliver your weekly digest
  • Medical specialty — to send you the correct specialty digest(s)
  • Payment information — processed securely by Stripe; we never see or store your card details

We do not collect your name, NHS number, workplace, browsing history, or any other personal information.

How we use your data

Your email address and specialty selection are used solely to:

  • Send you your weekly Monday Clinical Brief digest email(s)
  • Send occasional service-related communications (e.g. changes to the service, renewal notices)

We will never sell, rent, or share your email address with third parties. We do not send marketing emails on behalf of other organisations. We do not use your data for advertising.

Lawful basis for processing

We process your data on the basis of contractual necessity — you provide your email and specialty so that we can fulfil your subscription and deliver the digest service you have paid for.

Payment processing

All payments are handled by Stripe, a PCI-DSS compliant payment processor. When you subscribe, you are redirected to Stripe's secure checkout page. We do not receive, process, or store your credit or debit card details at any point. Stripe's own privacy policy governs their handling of your payment data.

How we store your data

Your email address and specialty selection are stored on a password-protected, encrypted device with access restricted to the service operator only. Your email address is also held within our email sending service which is protected by two-factor authentication.

We do not store your data on shared servers or third-party platforms beyond what is necessary to deliver the service. We retain your data for the duration of your subscription. If you cancel, we will delete your data within 30 days of your subscription ending.

AI-generated content

Our journal summaries are generated using artificial intelligence (Anthropic Claude). Article metadata (titles, authors, abstracts) is retrieved from PubMed, a public database maintained by the U.S. National Library of Medicine.

Your personal data is never sent to any AI service or third-party API. The AI processes only publicly available journal article data — never subscriber information.

Your rights

Under UK GDPR, you have the following rights:

AccessRequest a copy of the personal data we hold about you
RectificationAsk us to correct inaccurate data
ErasureAsk us to delete your data ("right to be forgotten")
PortabilityReceive your data in a machine-readable format
ObjectObject to processing of your data
Withdraw consentWhere processing is based on consent

To exercise any of these rights, email us at info@mondayclinicalbrief.co.uk. We will respond within 30 days.

Unsubscribing

You can unsubscribe at any time by:

  • Clicking the "Unsubscribe" link at the bottom of any digest email
  • Emailing us at info@mondayclinicalbrief.co.uk

Your subscription payment can be cancelled through Stripe. Once cancelled, you will not be charged again at the next renewal date.

Cookies

Our website does not use cookies or any tracking technologies. We do not use analytics services such as Google Analytics. The Stripe checkout page may set its own cookies, governed by Stripe's cookie policy.

Changes to this policy

We may update this policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. If we make significant changes, we will notify subscribers by email.

Complaints

If you are unhappy with how we have handled your data, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO): ico.org.uk/make-a-complaint.

Home Privacy Policy Terms of Service Contact

© 2026 The Monday Clinical Brief
